name: Robust Italian Stew: Wine-Braised Beef Over C
recipeIngredient: 2 1/4 cups Beef Stock or Canned Broth
recipeInstructions:
Italian Stew:
1. Heat oil in a heavy Dutch oven over high heat. Add 1/5 of the beef and brown on all sides, stirring occasionally, about 5 minutes. Transfer beef to a bowl using a slotted spoon. Repeat with remaining beef in 4 more batches.
2. Reduce heat to medium. Add onion and Italian parsley to the Dutch oven and cook until onion is golden brown, stirring frequently, about 10 minutes.
3. Stir in chopped garlic, bay leaves, ground cloves, cinnamon, and allspice.
4. Return beef to the Dutch oven and stir to coat with onion and spice mixture.
5. Add red wine and bring to boil, scraping up any browned bits. Simmer mixture for 15 minutes.
6. Add beef stock and simmer for 10 minutes. Add tomatoes with their juices, breaking up tomatoes with the back of a spoon. Mix in olives and rosemary.
7. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until beef is tender, stirring occasionally, about 1 1/2 hours. Add bell peppers, cover, and cook until just tender, about 15 minutes.
8. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Polenta:
1. Bring water and salt to boil in a heavy large saucepan. Gradually whisk in cornmeal. Continue whisking until the mixture boils and thickens, about 2 minutes. Reduce heat to low.
2. Cook cornmeal until thick and thoroughly cooked, whisking occasionally, about 30 minutes.
3. (Can be prepared up to 4 hours ahead. Transfer polenta to a metal bowl. Cover tightly with aluminum foil. Set the bowl over a large saucepan of barely-simmering water. Stir occasionally.)
